explanation:
**Personal finance and investment** use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard industry formulas.

## How It's Calculated

How interest rate changes affect your savings.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Typical Benchmarks by Time Period

| Time Period | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Stick to the plan** during market downturns. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: annual fees >1% reduce your 30-year returns by 30%.
- **Long time horizon**: stocks work best over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Annual rebalancing**: once per year is enough.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Data is current to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How do interest rate changes affect my savings account?
faq.a: When central bank interest rates rise, savings account yields typically increase. This calculator shows you the exact impact on your savings based on the new rate and your time horizon.

faq.q: What is the BCRA and why does it matter?
faq.a: The BCRA (Central Bank of Argentina) sets the reference interest rate. Changes to this rate affect savings accounts, loans, and investments throughout Argentina's financial system.

faq.q: How do I use this calculator?
faq.a: Enter your savings amount, the time period in months, and the interest rate (%). The calculator instantly shows how your savings will grow and provides an interpretation of the results.
Results are approximate within ±5%. We use standard formulas validated internationally. For critical financial decisions, consult a professional advisor.

faq.q: Is my data stored or shared?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send your data to any server and don't store personal information.

faq.q: How often is the data updated?
faq.a: Our formulas are current as of 2026. We review data quarterly to ensure accuracy and relevance.
Yes, all Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free and don't require registration.

faq.q: What's the difference between nominal and real interest rates?
faq.a: Nominal rate is what the bank advertises. Real rate accounts for inflation. Real rate = nominal rate minus inflation rate. Your purchasing power grows at the real rate.

faq.q: Can I use this for both savings and loans?
faq.a: Yes. Enter your loan or savings amount and the interest rate. The formula works for both scenarios—positive rates show investment growth, negative scenarios show loan costs.
